2605.13415.title: Continual Learning with Multilingual Foundation Model
2605.13415.paper_summary: This paper presents a multi-stage framework for detecting reclaimed slurs in multilingual social media discourse. It addresses the challenge of identifying reclamatory versus non-reclamatory usage of LGBTQ+-related slurs across English, Spanish, and Italian tweets. The framework handles three intertwined methodological challenges like data scarcity, class imbalance, and cross-linguistic variation in sentiment expression. It integrates data-driven model selection via cross-validation, semantic-preserving augmentation through back-translation, inductive transfer learning with dynamic epoch-level undersampling, and domain-specific knowledge injection via masked language modeling. Eight multilingual embedding models were evaluated systematically, with XLM-RoBERTa selected as the foundation model based on macro-averaged F1 score. Data augmentation via GPT-4o-mini back-translation to alternate languages effectively tripled the training corpus while preserving semantic content and class distribution ratios. The framework produces four final runs for the evaluation purposes where RUN 1 is inductive transfer learning with augmentation and undersampling, RUN 2 with masked language modeling pre-training, RUN 3 and RUN 4 are previous predictions refined via language-specific decision thresholds optimized via ROC analysis. Language-specific threshold refinement reveals that optimal decision boundaries vary significantly across languages. This reflects distributional differences in model confidence scores and linguistic variation in reclamatory language usage. The threshold-based optimization yields 2-5% absolute F1 improvement without requiring model retraining. The methodology is fully reproducible, with all code and experimental setup available at https://github.com/rbg-research/MultiPRIDE-Evalita-2026.
2605.13415.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文提出了一個多階段框架，用於檢測多語言社交媒體話語中的回收污名。它解決了識別與 LGBTQ+ 相關的污名在英語、西班牙語和意大利語推文中回收性使用與非回收性使用的挑戰。該框架處理三個相互交織的方法論挑戰，如數據稀缺、類別不平衡以及情感表達的跨語言變異。它通過交叉驗證整合了數據驅動的模型選擇、通過回譯進行的語義保留增強、動態時期級別的欠抽樣的歸納轉移學習，以及通過掩碼語言建模進行的領域特定知識注入。八個多語言嵌入模型被系統性評估，基於宏觀平均 F1 分數選擇 XLM-RoBERTa 作為基礎模型。通過 GPT-4o-mini 回譯到其他語言的數據增強有效地將訓練語料庫增加了三倍，同時保留了語義內容和類別分佈比率。該框架生成了四個最終運行以供評估，其中 RUN 1 是帶有增強和欠抽樣的歸納轉移學習，RUN 2 是帶有掩碼語言建模預訓練，RUN 3 和 RUN 4 是通過語言特定決策閾值優化的先前預測。語言特定閾值的細化顯示，最佳決策邊界在不同語言之間存在顯著差異。這反映了模型置信分數的分佈差異和回收語言使用的語言變異。基於閾值的優化在不需要模型重新訓練的情況下實現了 2-5% 的絕對 F1 改進。該方法論是完全可重現的，所有代碼和實驗設置可在 https://github.com/rbg-research/MultiPRIDE-Evalita-2026 獲得。
2605.13415.author: Barathi Ganesh HB et.al.
2605.13415.authors: Barathi Ganesh HB, Michal Ptaszynski, Rene Melendez, Juuso Eronen
